positions.label: Treno tournament & the fall of Alexandria
Crystal World — point of no return

items.name: Sword fight — impress 100 nobles
items.location: Prima Vista performance — 'I Want To Be Your Canary'
items.notes: Nail the button prompts during the Blank duel; 100 impressed nobles earns a reward from the Queen later.

items.name: Jump rope — King of Jump Rope
items.location: Alexandria square — as Vivi (Disc 1) or Eiko (Disc 3)
items.notes: 1000 jumps for the King of Jump Rope key item. Alexandria's square is gone after the fall.

items.name: Evil Forest sweep
items.location: Evil Forest — before it petrifies
items.notes: The whole forest turns to stone when you leave. Grab the chests and the spring scene first.

items.name: Stiltzkin's bundles — every meeting
items.location: Traveling moogle — appears across the whole game
items.notes: Buy his discount bundle at every encounter; the final Memoria meeting sells a Ribbon.

items.name: Ragtime Mouse quiz
items.location: Random encounters in forests, all discs
items.notes: Answer his true/false theater trivia across the game; full marks pays out on Disc 4.

items.name: Morrid's three coffees
items.location: Moccha (observatory area), Kirman (Madain Sari), Burman (Dali windmill)
items.notes: Deliver all three to Morrid's tower for the Mini-Prima Vista. Windows on individual coffees vary — verify.

items.name: Friendly Mu
items.location: Plains around Dali
items.notes: First of the friendly monster chain — give it an Ore. The chain must be done in order.

items.name: Festival of the Hunt
items.location: Lindblum
items.notes: Winner and prize depend on who scores highest — Zidane's win pays the most gil.

items.name: Recruit Quina
items.location: Qu's Marsh — catch a frog with Quina watching
items.notes: Skippable at the marsh; the story eventually forces the issue. Take her early for Blue Magic.

items.name: Frog catching — 99 frogs
items.location: Every Qu's Marsh in the world
items.notes: Long-haul minigame. Rewards scale with the count; the 99th brings Quale's challenge.

items.name: Gastro Fork (Quina)
items.location: Quale's challenge — after 99 frogs
items.notes: Quina's ultimate weapon, from beating Quale.

items.name: Moguta's Kupo Nuts
items.location: Gizamaluke's Grotto — feed Moguta a Kupo Nut each visit
items.notes: Return with a fresh Kupo Nut per story chunk for escalating rewards.

items.name: Chocobo Hot & Cold — meet Choco
items.location: Chocobo's Forest — between Lindblum and the marsh
items.notes: FF9's whole endgame economy hides behind this minigame. Start digging.

route.why: The chocograph chain starts here — everything below needs Choco

items.name: Reef Choco (light blue)
items.location: Chocobo's Forest — dig up the shallows chocographs
items.notes: First field-ability upgrade: Choco can wade shallows.

route.why: First ability upgrade — unlocks the lagoon

items.name: Mountain Choco (red)
items.location: Chocograph treasures — forest and lagoon digs
items.notes: Cliffs and crags open up. Keep digging chocographs between story beats.

route.why: Second upgrade — cliffs stop being walls

items.name: Ocean Choco (dark blue)
items.location: Chocobo's Lagoon — deeper chocograph treasures
items.notes: Full ocean crossing. The world map is now yours.

route.why: Open ocean — most ultimate-weapon digs need this

items.name: Dead Pepper treasure spots
items.location: Bubbling ocean patches and cracks — fed to Choco
items.notes: Dead Peppers dig the underwater and mountainside caches — several ultimate weapons live here.

route.why: The Dead Pepper caches hold half the endgame gear

items.name: Sky (Gold) Choco
items.location: Final chocograph chain reward
items.notes: Flight. FF9's gold chocobo moment.

route.why: Flight — the Air Garden is the point

items.name: Chocobo's Air Garden
items.location: Shadow on the world map — land with the Sky Choco
items.notes: The floating garden with the last chocographs — and Ozma.

route.why: The final digs, and the superboss lives upstairs

items.name: All 24 chocographs
items.location: Forest, Lagoon, and Air Garden digs
items.notes: Completion sweep of every chocograph treasure.

route.why: Mop up the map while the abilities are fresh

items.name: Ozma (superboss)
items.location: Chocobo's Air Garden — the eidolon cave
items.notes: FF9's Ruby/Emerald. Finish the friendly monster chain first if you want to hit it with physicals.

route.why: The loadout is ready — go say hello

items.name: Learn Ark (Pumice)
items.location: Combine the Pumice — Ozma drop / auction pieces
items.notes: Dagger's hidden ultimate eidolon, assembled from Pumice.

route.why: Ozma's drop finishes the set

items.name: Burmecia sweep
items.location: Burmecia — the ruined city
items.notes: Window set pessimistically — verify whether the city reopens later.

items.name: Cleyra goodies before the fall
items.location: Cleyra — settlement and trunk
items.notes: The whole town is erased by Odin. Anything not taken is gone forever.

route.why: The town does not survive this disc

items.name: 12 Stellazzio coins
items.location: Hidden across the world — turn in to Queen Stella, Treno
items.notes: Rewards per coin turned in — the Blood Sword for Steiner arrives at five.

items.name: Ophiuchus — the 13th coin
items.location: Quan's Dwelling (Disc 4), then Queen Stella
items.notes: Appears only after all twelve are turned in. The Hammer it earns feeds the secret ending.

route.why: The Hammer is the secret-ending ticket

items.name: Treno weapon shop battles
items.location: Treno — monster in the weapon shop basement, new each disc
items.notes: A different beatable monster per disc with a prize each. Earlier discs' monsters are lost as time moves on.

items.name: Treno auction rares
items.location: Treno auction house
items.notes: Buy the rare key items as they rotate in — the Magical Fingertip chain ends in Excalibur.

items.name: Friendly Ghost
items.location: Plains around Treno
items.notes: Wants an Ore. Chain order is fixed.

items.name: Tantarian (hidden boss)
items.location: Alexandria Castle library — the moving book
items.notes: Only while Alexandria Castle stands — the fall of Alexandria ends it. Drops the Running Shoes.

items.name: Ramuh's tale (Peridot)
items.location: Pinnacle Rocks — assemble Ramuh's story
items.notes: Pick the story ending for the Peridot and Dagger's Ramuh.

items.name: Friendly Ladybug
items.location: Eunorus Plains — near the Black Mage Village
items.notes: Wants two Ores.

items.name: Friendly Yeti
items.location: Forgotten Continent
items.notes: Wants two Diamonds.

items.name: Friendly Nymph
items.location: Iifa Tree area
items.notes: Wants three Ores.

items.name: Hippaul racing (Vivi)
items.location: Alexandria main street — race Hippaul to level 80
items.notes: Only during the coronation stretch. Level 80 pays the Protect Ring.

route.why: Gone the moment the story leaves Alexandria

items.name: Dali Mayor's Key
items.location: Dali — sleeping mayor, then the windmill
items.notes: Pickpocket the key while he naps; the windmill hides the Mini-Brahne and the Burman coffee.

items.name: Desert Palace sweep
items.location: Kuja's Desert Palace — bluish light chests
items.notes: One visit only. Light every lamp; the Promist Ring and friends are unrepeatable.

route.why: You never come back here

items.name: Oeilvert sweep
items.location: Oeilvert — the anti-magic facility
items.notes: One visit only. Physical-team dungeon; don't leave chests behind.

route.why: Same trip, same rule — one pass

items.name: Mount Gulug sweep
items.location: Mount Gulug — via Esto Gaza
items.notes: Red Hat, Golden Hairpin, and the last unrepeatable chests before the disc turns.

items.name: Daguerreo — the hidden library
items.location: Island grotto — southwest seas
items.notes: Synth shop, rare stock, and the four-armed man who rates your treasure hunting.

route.why: Best shops in the game — stock up here from now on

items.name: Friendly Jabberwock
items.location: Seaways Canyon — near Daguerreo
items.notes: Wants an Emerald.

items.name: Friendly Feather Circle
items.location: Healing Shore — Forgotten Continent
items.notes: Wants a Moonstone.

items.name: Friendly Garuda
items.location: Vile Island region
items.notes: Wants a Lapis Lazuli.

items.name: Friendly Yan — chain finale
items.location: Vile Island
items.notes: Wants a Diamond. Completes the chain: Ribbon, Rebirth Ring — and Ozma becomes reachable with physical attacks.

route.why: Finish the chain before stepping to Ozma

items.name: Whale Whisker (Dagger)
items.location: Chocograph treasure — ocean dig
items.notes: Dagger's ultimate rod, out of a chocograph cache.

items.name: Angel Flute (Eiko)
items.location: Chocograph treasure — late chain
items.notes: Eiko's ultimate flute from the final chocograph stretch.

items.name: Ragnarok (Steiner)
items.location: Chocograph treasure — Outer Island dig
items.notes: Steiner's second-best sword — the best one has a clock on it.

items.name: Ipsen's Castle sweep
items.location: Ipsen's Castle — the inverted keep
items.notes: Weaker weapons hit harder here. The Ancient Aroma and Taharka's drops are the prizes.

items.name: The Invincible
items.location: Story — Disc 4 begins
items.notes: Full world access. Every remaining sidequest is open from here.

route.why: Disc 4 — the mop-up disc starts now

items.name: Mognet Central & the Superslick
items.location: Mognet Central — snowy island (Sky Choco or Invincible)
items.notes: Fix the mail machine via the letter chain; Superslick and a Protect Ring for the trouble.

route.why: The letter chain resolves only from here

items.name: Madain Sari — Eidolon Wall secret
items.location: Madain Sari — walk the wall the long way
items.notes: Circle the Eidolon Wall the hidden direction for the mural scenes and reward.

route.why: Two minutes of walking for one of FF9's best secrets

items.name: Ultima Weapon (Zidane)
items.location: Dead Pepper dive — the sunken Shimmering Island spot
items.notes: Zidane's ultimate dagger, under the sea where Terra's portal was.

route.why: The dive only exists after Terra

items.name: Dragon's Hair (Freya)
items.location: Dead Pepper cache — ocean shipwreck
items.notes: Freya's ultimate spear from a Dead Pepper dig.

items.name: Tiger Racket (Dagger)
items.location: Dead Pepper cache — Quan's Dwelling coast
items.notes: Her best racket — the rod is still the Whale Whisker.

items.name: Excalibur (Steiner)
items.location: Daguerreo — old man, for the Magical Fingertip
items.notes: Buy rares at the auction until the Magical Fingertip rotates in, then trade it in Daguerreo.

items.name: Nero Family minigame
items.location: Alexandria — Tantalus hideout, during Disc 4 story beats
items.notes: Shuffle-game with the brothers between Memoria detours; small but famously obscure.

items.name: Treasure Hunter Rank S
items.location: Daguerreo — the four-armed appraiser
items.notes: Open enough chests worldwide and he crowns you Rank S.

items.name: Excalibur II — under 12:00:00
items.location: Memoria — near the Lich fight, only if play time < 12 hours
items.notes: The most infamous timed item in the series. A dedicated speed-run playthrough, not a pickup.

route.why: Only exists on a sub-12-hour clock — know before you go

items.name: Mace of Zeus (Vivi)
items.location: Memoria depths — last stretch before the Crystal World
items.notes: Vivi's ultimate weapon, missable in the final dungeon's last leg.

route.why: Last chance is literally the final corridor

items.name: Rune Claws (Amarant)
items.location: Memoria depths
items.notes: Amarant's ultimate weapon, also buried in Memoria's endgame.

route.why: Same corridor, same rule
